title: leontodon × transsilvanicus
intro: Leontodon × transsilvanicus, also known as Transylvanian hawk's beard, is a hybrid plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) resulting from the cross between Leontodon hispidus and Leontodon tuberosus. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Leontodon × transsilvanicus has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, respiratory disorders, and skin conditions, thanks to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and creams for topical application.
